_comment: llm-lab default config. DO NOT EDIT: create ./config.json with your overrides and run '.\llm-lab.ps1 init' to populate hardware detection.
_comment_exe: Absolute path to llama-server.exe (CUDA build recommended). If null, the tool will try to auto-detect in PATH and in sibling folders of scan_paths.
_comment_scan: List of absolute paths to recursively scan for .gguf files. Example: ['C:/models', 'D:/llm']. Configured via 'init' or manually.
_comment_dense: Family names (exact, case-sensitive) that the MoE-detection regex (`A\d+B`, `MoE`, `Mixtral`) matches but are actually dense. Add families here to force them to a Tier A or C plan sweep instead of the wasted Tier B `--n-cpu-moe` sweep. Example: ["something-A100B-special"].

hardware._comment_budget: Fraction of VRAM considered safe from WDDM paging (Windows-specific). Empirical: 0.95 on a desktop with browser open.

bench.prompt: Write a Python function that computes the Fibonacci sequence recursively with memoization. Include an explanatory docstring and a usage example.
bench._comment_warmup: Run one small completion with identical prompt and cache_prompt=true before the real bench, to compile CUDA graphs for all batch sizes in use. Without this the first run has a 10-20s compile penalty that pollutes prompt_per_second.

_comment_base: Flags applied to every benchmark. --flash-attn auto is safer than 'on' (falls back on builds that don't support it). Thread counts are injected dynamically from hardware.cpu_cores_*.

tier_classification._comment_overhead: Approximate fixed VRAM overhead beyond model weights: compute buffers (~500), recurrent/SSM state (~50-200), mmproj (~900 if present), graph+misc (~100). Used to decide A vs C.

_comment_candidates: Context / KV-quant combos to try for Tier A models. q8_0 -> near-lossless, q4_0 -> unlocks ~2x more ctx at minor quality hit.

wddm_detection._comment_sat: If peak_vram / total_vram exceeds this, config is flagged as 'WDDM risk'.
wddm_detection._comment_drop: Within a family, if eval_tps < drop_threshold * max_eval_tps_in_family, flag as 'WDDM paging suspected'.
wddm_detection._comment_counter: Poll Windows Perf Counter '\GPU Adapter Memory(*)\Shared Usage' during bench. Only a delta (above baseline) greater than shared_delta_confirm_mib is considered a confirmed WDDM paging event -- smaller drifts are background noise from other GPU consumers (browser, Discord).
